engaged_works.title: The Philosophy of Integralism
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Routed from the 2026-05-10 Tier-1 publication backlog as an on-disk source witness.
engaged_works.summary: This on-disk text is being wired into the Source tab as a citation witness for Haridas Chaudhuri, The Philosophy of Integralism. The entry is intentionally minimal: it records the routed file and basic bibliographic shape so the site can surface the source while fuller review waits. A later pass should tighten the title, edition, and passage coverage against the on-disk witness.

core_thesis: Chaudhuri's claim is that Integral Yoga requires a pūrṇa-advaita in which Brahman is grasped not as a static One behind becoming but as the undivided unity of the formless and its self-expressive powers. Evolution is intelligible only because consciousness is already involved in matter and progressively resumes itself through life, mind, and integral transformation ([Integral Yoga, ch. 1](cite://chaudhuri/integral-yoga/1); [The Evolution of Integral Consciousness, ch. 2](cite://chaudhuri/the-evolution-of-integral-consciousness/2)).
